howItWorks.intro: This attorney fee calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Fees = Hourly Rate × Estimated Hours
Retainer is an upfront deposit deducted from total
Average attorney rates: $200-500/hr depending on specialty and market
howItWorks.outro: Laws vary by jurisdiction and change frequently. This is not legal advice — consult a licensed attorney.

faq.question: How much does a lawyer cost per hour?
faq.answer: Average: $200-400/hr. Small town: $150-250. Major city: $300-700+. Specialty matters: family law $200-350, corporate $400-700, IP $350-550. Many offer free initial consultations.

faq.question: What is a retainer fee?
faq.answer: A retainer is an upfront deposit held in a trust account. The lawyer bills against it hourly. Once depleted, you'll be billed directly. Typical retainers range from $2,000-10,000 depending on case complexity.

faq.question: Are there alternatives to hourly billing?
faq.answer: Yes: flat fee (common for simple matters), contingency fee (33-40% of settlement — common in personal injury), and hybrid arrangements. Always discuss fee structure before hiring.

faq.question: Is the Attorney Fee Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard legal matters formulas trusted by individuals navigating legal issues. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Attorney Fee Calculator?
sections.body: The Attorney Fee Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals navigating legal issues who need quick, accurate calculations in the legal matters space. By entering your hourly rate, estimated hours, retainer fee, you get instant results including estimated total fees, due after retainer, monthly. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting estimated total fees right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In legal matters, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals navigating legal issues worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to understand your legal situation with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your hourly rate and need to find the right estimated total fees. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
